title: City Council Regular Session Meeting 7/7/2026
agendaText: City Council Regular Session Meeting - July 07, 2026 Agenda Full Package Agenda Full Package Open Video in new Window Video City Council Agenda City of Campbell, 70 N. First St., Campbell, California NOTE: This City Council Regular meeting will be conducted in person as well as telecommunication and is compliant with provisions of the Brown Act. The City Council meeting will be live-streamed on Channel 26 (Xfinity) or Channel 88 (Direct TV), the City's website and on YouTube https://www.youtube.com/user/CityofCampbell for those who only wish to view the meeting. Those members of the public wishing to provide public comment virtually are asked to register in advance at: https://www.campbellca.gov/signup . After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting. Members of the public may attend the meeting in person at Campbell City Hall - Council Chambers. Public comment will also be accepted via email at [email protected] until 12:00 p.m. prior to the start of the meeting. Written comments will be posted on the website and distributed to the Council. If you choose to email your comments, please indicate in the subject line “FOR PUBLIC COMMENT” and indicate the item number. REGULAR MEETING OF THE CAMPBELL CITY COUNCIL Tuesday, July 07, 2026 07:00 PM City Hall Council Chamber 1. CALL TO ORDER/ROLL CALL 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE 3. SPECIAL PRESENTATIONS AND PROCLAMATIONS Please Note: Items listed under Special Presentations and Proclamations are considered ceremonial. The City Council will not take action on these matters. 3.1 Proclamation Declaring July 2026 as Park and Recreation Month 1. Proclamation Declaring July 2026 as Park and Recreation Month.pdf 2. Proclamation - PRM 2026.pdf Recommended Action That the Mayor issue a proclamation declaring the month of July 2026 as National Park and Recreation Month for the City of Campbell. 4. COMMUNICATIONS AND PETITIONS 5. ORAL REQUESTS NOTE: This portion of the meeting is reserved for persons wishing to address the City Council on any matter not on the agenda. Persons wishing to address the Council are requested, but not required to complete a Speaker’s Card. Speakers are limited to two (2) minutes. The law generally prohibits the Council from discussion or taking action on such items. However, the Council may instruct staff accordingly regarding Oral Requests. 6. COUNCIL ANNOUNCEMENTS 7. CONSENT CALENDAR NOTE: All matters listed under consent calendar are considered by the City Council to be routine and will be enacted by one motion. There will be no separate discussion of these items unless a request is made by a member of City Council, City staff, or a member of the public. Any person wishing to speak on any item on the consent calendar should ask to have the item removed from the consent calendar prior to the time the Council votes to approve. If removed, the item will be discussed in the order in which it appears. 7.1 Minutes of City Council Executive Session Meeting of June 16, 2026 1. Minutes - Package City Council Executive Session _Jun16_2026.pdf Recommended Action Approve the executive session meeting minutes of June 16, 2026. 7.2 Minutes of City Council Regular Meeting of June 16, 2026 1. PostMinutes - Package City Council Regular Session Meeting_Jun16_2026.pdf Recommended Action Approve the regular meeting minutes of June 16, 2026. 7.3 Approving Payment of Bills and Claims 1. Report-Approving Bills and Claims 07.07.26.pdf Recommended Action Approve the bills and claims in the amount of $3,439,308.69. 7.4 Monthly Investment Transactions Report- May 2026 1. Report Monthly Investment Transactions Report- May 2026.pdf 2. Monthly Investment tranx rpt- May'26-2.pdf 3. Chandler BNY Investment Statement- 5.31.26.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council review and accept the Monthly Investment Transactions Report for May 2026. 7.5 Monthly Treasurer’s Report (May 2026) 1. Report - Monthly Treasurer's Report May 2026.pdf 2. 2026.05 Fund Balance Report.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council review and accept the Monthly Treasurer’s Report for the month ended May 31, 2026. 7.6 Adoption of Ordinance 26-2353 Adjusting City Council Compensation 1. Report - Adoption of Ordinance 26-2353 Adjusting City Council Compensation.pdf 2. Ordinance 26-2353 - City Council Compensation.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council take second reading of Ordinance 26-2353 adjusting City Council Compensation effective January 2027. 7.7 Approve an agreement with HdL Companies for sales or transaction and use tax services, authorize the City Manager to execute the agreement, and dispense with bidding procedures (Resolution/Roll Call Vote)​ 1. Report - HdL Sales Tax Contract.pdf 2. Resolution HdL Contract (eff. 07-01-26).pdf 3. HdL Sales Tax Agreement (eff 07-01-26).pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution approving an agreement, authorizing the City Manager to execute a five-year Agreement with Hinderliter, De Llamas & Associates (HdL Companies) to provide Sales or Transaction and Use Tax Audit Services and Sales or Transaction and Use Tax Management Services, and dispensing with the bidding procedures specified in Campbell Municipal Code (CMC) Section 3.20.050. 7.8 Resolution Approving the Revision of the Recreation Coordinator, Community Services Officer, and Street and Fleet Maintenance Supervisor Classification Specifications, and the Addition of a New Classification Specification for Lead Mechanic (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. 2026.07.07 Budget Classification Updates.pdf 2. Resolution - Revised Job Specifications for Recreation Coordinator, Community Services Officer, and Street and Fleet Maintenance Supervisor and New Specification for Lead Mechanic.pdf 3. Recreation Program Coordinator Classification Specification - Redline.pdf 4. Recreation Program Coordinator Classification Specification - Final.pdf 5. Community Services Officer Classification Specification - Redline.pdf 6. Community Services Officer Classification Specification - Final.pdf 7. Street and Fleet Maintenance Supervisor Classification Specification - Redline.pdf 8. Street and Fleet Maintenance Supervisor Final.pdf 9. Lead Mechanic Classification Specification.pdf Recommended Action Adopt a resolution approving the revised classification specifications for Recreation Coordinator, Community Services Officer, and Street and Fleet Maintenance Supervisor, and a new classification specification for Lead Mechanic. 7.9 Approval of Memorandum of Understanding between City of Campbell and Campbell Municipal Employees Association (CMEA) (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Approval of Memorandum of Understanding Between City of Campbell and Campbell Municipal Employees Association (CMEA).pdf 2. Resolution - CMEA 2026 Approving MOU 06.05.2026.pdf 3. CMEA MOU 2026-2028_Final Draft to CMEA_07.07.2026_v2.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution ratifying a successor Memorandum of Understanding, effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2028, between the City of Campbell and the Campbell Municipal Employees Association (CMEA). 8. PUBLIC HEARINGS AND INTRODUCTIONS OF ORDINANCES NOTE: Members of the public may be allotted up to two (2) minutes to comment on any public hearing item. Applicants/Appellants and their representatives may be allotted up to a total of five (5) minutes for opening statements and up to a total of three (3) minutes maximum for closing statements. Items requested/recommended for continuance are subject to Council’s consent at the meeting. 8.1 Lighting and Landscaping District LLA-1, Fiscal Year 2026-27 – Confirming Assessments (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - LLA-1 FY26-27 Confirming Assessments.pdf 2. Resolution LLA FY26-27.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution overruling protests, if any, approving the Engineer’s Report, ordering the improvements, and confirming the diagram and assessments. 8.2 Public Hearing to consider adoption of an interim ordinance superseding prior interim ordinance implementing Senate Bill (SB) 450 (2024), as modified by Assembly Bill (AB) 1061 (2025), to revise historic resource eligibility provisions applicable to two-unit developments and urban lot splits, simplify subdivision regulations, and eliminate design standards for certain Single-Family Residential Zoning Districts (Ordinance/ 4/5 roll call vote). 1. Report - Adoption of an Interim Ordinance Superseding a Prior Interim Ordinance Implementing Senate Bill (SB) 450 (2024), as Modified by Assembly Bill (AB) 1061 (2025).pdf 2. Draft Ordinance.pdf 3. Redline Ordinance (for reference only).pdf 4. HPB Memo.pdf 5. Public Comment.pdf Recommended Action It is recommended that the City Council adopt an interim ordinance superseding Interim Ordinance No. 2321 and revising regulations applicable to single-family residential development and urban lot splits. 8.3 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of a Resolution revoking the Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for California Cigar and More #3 located at 2145 South Winchester Blvd, Campbell. (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Revocation of Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for California Cigar and More.pdf 2. Resolution - Revocation Campbell Cigar and More.pdf 3. Incident Report 260120053-1 California Cigar and More_Redacted.pdf 4. Incident Report 260112014-1 (Stay Vaped) California Cigar and More.pdf 5. Appeal (2026.06.17) California Cigar and More.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution revoking the Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for California Cigar and More #3, located at 2145 South Winchester Boulevard, Campbell, CA; and deny the appeal. 8.4 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of a Resolution revoking the Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for Haze Downtown Smoke Shop, located at 469 East Campbell, Campbell. (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Revocation of Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for Haze Downtown Smoke Shop.pdf 2. Resolution - Revocation Haze Downtown Smoke Shop.pdf 3. Incident Report 250305016-1 (Haze Downtown).pdf 4. Haze_469_E_Campbell_Appeal.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution revoking the Tobacco Retailer Permit and Business License for Haze Downtown Smoke Shop, located at 469 East Campbell, CA; and deny the appeal. 8.5 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of a Resolution revoking the Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for Haze Smoke Shop, located at 2050 South Bascom, Campbell (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Revocation of Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for Haze Smoke Shop.pdf 2. Resolution - Revocation Haze Smoke Shop.pdf 3. Incident Report 250305044-1 (Haze Smoke).pdf 4. Haze_2050_S_Bascom_Appeal.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution revoking the Tobacco Retailer Permit and Business License for Haze Smoke Shop, located at 2050 South Bascom, Campbell and deny the appeal. 8.6 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of a Resolution revoking the Business License for Sage Smoke Shop, located at 484 West Hamilton Avenue, Campbell (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Revocation of Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for Sage Smoke Shop.pdf 2. Resolution - Revocation Sage Smoke Shop.pdf 3. Incident Report 250305049-1 (Sage Smoke Shop).pdf 4. Incident Report 260120035-1 (Sage Smoke Shop).pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution revoking the Business License for Sage Smoke Shop, located at 484 West Hamilton Avenue, Campbell. 8.7 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of a Resolution revoking the Business License for Dreamy Exotics located at 835 Union Avenue, Campbell. (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Revocation of Business License and Tobacco Retailer Permit for Dreamy Exotics.pdf 2. Resolution - Revocation Dreamy Exotics.pdf 3. Incident Report 250222021-1 (Dreamy Exotic Smoke Shop).pdf 4. Incident Report
